_notes: Negation targets MUST be additive/modular objects that can plausibly be absent from a scene without violating physical priors. Structural elements (walls, floors, ceilings, sky, ground) are EXCLUDED — 'no floor' tests physical prior override, not logical negation processing. Each scene must have a realistic default expectation that such objects are often present, so saying 'no X' is a meaningful constraint on an otherwise typical scene.

scene_filler_phrases: a kitchen with marble countertops and warm afternoon light
a bright modern kitchen with wooden cabinets
a cozy kitchen with tiled floors and a sunny window
a tidy kitchen with stone counters and hanging pendant lights
a bedroom with soft morning light and pale blue walls
a small bedroom with wooden flooring and a tall window
a neat bedroom with cream walls and patterned linens
a minimalist bedroom with light wood furniture
a quiet suburban street in the late afternoon
a narrow city street with cobblestone pavement
a residential street lined with trees
a wide main street with shop fronts on both sides
a modern office room with large windows and light gray walls
a clean workstation in an open-plan office
a small private office with a wooden desk
a bright corporate meeting room
a public park on a sunny weekend morning
a small urban park with paved paths and green lawns
a neighborhood park with benches and flower beds
a wide open park under a clear blue sky
